## feat: additional embed controls

This pull request introduces a sidebar to the embedded task map view, adds new configuration options for controlling the display of the view panel and tag visibility, and ensures these options are properly handled throughout the codebase. It also includes tests for the new configuration options and updates the styling to support the new sidebar layout.

Closes #500

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: small fixes for dropping tasks on a project

This pull request improves how task node group assignments and drag-over highlights are handled in the `TaskMapGraphView` component. The changes ensure that parent group information is always accurate by referencing the current state of nodes, which addresses inconsistencies that can occur during drag events. Additionally, the logic now prevents redundant project assignments when dragging tasks.

## fix(deps): update dependency i18next to v26.2.0

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Type | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| [i18next](https://www.i18next.com) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next)) | dependencies | minor | [`26.1.0` -> `26.2.0`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/i18next/26.1.0/26.2.0) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>i18next/i18next (i18next)</summary>

### [`v26.2.0`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2620)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.1.0...v26.2.0)

-   feat(types): new `parseInterpolation` TypeOption (default `true`). When set to `false` in `CustomTypeOptions`, the type-level extractor stops parsing translation strings for `{{variable}}` patterns. Required by `i18next-icu` users — the default extractor mistakes ICU MessageFormat nested-brace plurals like `{count, plural, one {{count} row} other {{count} rows}}` for an interpolation block and demands a phantom variable name. The flag is type-only; runtime interpolation is governed by `InterpolationOptions` and is unaffected. Fixes [i18next-icu#85](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next-icu/issues/85).
-   fix(types): expose `enableSelector` on `InitOptions` so `i18next.init({ enableSelector: 'strict' })` typechecks without a module augmentation. The runtime already reads `opts?.enableSelector` from init options; this lands the matching type declaration next to the other selector-resolution knobs. Accepts `false | true | 'optimize' | 'strict'`. Thanks [@​Faithfinder](https://redirect.github.com/Faithfinder) ([#​2431](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/pull/2431))

</details>

## fix: remove unintended obfuscation

The obsidian plugin scanner is warning about potential code obfuscation that can lead towards arbitrary code execution. This appears to come from React19. As we're not using functionality specific to React19 itself, and React18 is still getting security updates for the foreseeable future, downgrading seems like the right approach here.

Fixing more issues identified by the Obsidian plugin scanning system. In particular some CSS duplication.

## fix(deps): use suggested alternative packages

This PR addresses the Obsidian plugin code scanning issues related to available and recommended alternative packages.

This highlights a few other issues which have been reduced to warning for now. Will be addressed in future release.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: tag color manager

This pull request significantly refactors how tag colors are handled throughout the application. Instead of supporting "random" or "static" tag color modes, the system now uses a selectable color palette approach. Users can pick from several predefined color themes (palettes) for tag appearance, simplifying the codebase and improving consistency. The changes affect settings, tag rendering, CSS, and translations.

> [!WARNING]
> This technically is breaking on the settings, but released as `minor` since we're not yet at v1. The default is configured to `rainbow`.

Closes #436

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: improve styling logic upon dragging in project

This pull request refines the drag-and-drop highlighting behavior in the `TaskMapGraphView` to prevent project group nodes from being highlighted when a task is dragged over its current group. The main improvements ensure that groups a node already belongs to are excluded from drag-over highlighting, resulting in a more intuitive user experience.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: release with attestation

This pull request updates the release workflow to improve security and traceability by adding build provenance attestation. The changes ensure that the release process generates and attaches provenance information for key build artifacts, and grants the necessary permissions for this step.


This is in line with the new guidelines coming from https://community.obsidian.md

## fix(deps): update dependency i18next to v26.1.0

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Type | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| [i18next](https://www.i18next.com) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next)) | dependencies | minor | [`26.0.10` -> `26.1.0`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/i18next/26.0.10/26.1.0) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>i18next/i18next (i18next)</summary>

### [`v26.1.0`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2610)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.0.10...v26.1.0)

-   feat: `enableSelector: 'strict'` (TypeOptions + runtime option). Opt-in mode that drops the flattened-primary form from `NsResource` at the type level — every namespace (primary included) is exposed only under its own key on `$`, uniformly across single- and multi-ns hooks. At runtime, a leading selector path segment matching the scope's namespace list is always rewritten as a namespace prefix, including the primary. Eliminates the silent-miss surface area where `t($ => $.primary.foo)` typechecks but doesn't resolve under the default mode (see [#​2429](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2429)). Backward-compatible: default `enableSelector: false | true | 'optimize'` behavior is unchanged. Note: strict mode is incompatible with the [#​2405](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2405) pattern (keys whose names match sibling namespaces) — those users should stay on default mode.

</details>

---

## fix: minimap colors

This pull request updates the color variable names used for task statuses in the `TaskMinimap` component to use a more consistent naming scheme.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: better in-project layout for disjoint sub-graphs

Inside projects you can have multiple disjoint sub-graphs. The layout algorithm was not yet taking this into proper consideration. This is now fixed using a layout pass per-project before applying layout constraints related to cross- or out-of-project connections.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: multi-select nodes

This pull request enhances the drag-and-drop functionality within the task map graph, allowing users to assign multiple selected task nodes to a project group at once. It also improves the visual feedback during drag operations and adds support for multi-task assignment notices in several languages. 

Multi-select using an area is now done using shift+left-click to create a selection.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: drag and drop projects into project

This pull request adds support for assigning tasks to projects via drag-and-drop in the task map graph view. Now, when a user drags a task node over a project group node, the group is visually highlighted, and dropping the task into the group will assign it to that project. The changes also include user feedback via notices and localization for the new feature.

## fix(deps): update dependency i18next to v26.0.10

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Type | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| [i18next](https://www.i18next.com) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next)) | dependencies | patch | [`26.0.9` -> `26.0.10`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/i18next/26.0.9/26.0.10) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>i18next/i18next (i18next)</summary>

### [`v26.0.10`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#26010)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.0.9...v26.0.10)

-   feat: `getFixedT` accepts a fourth optional `fixedOpts` argument carrying `scopeNs` — the full namespace list the bound `t` was created for. The selector API uses `scopeNs` to detect when a path's first segment is a namespace prefix, **without** changing resolution scope. Resolution still uses the bound `ns` (a single primary string in the typical react-i18next setup), so plain `t('key')` lookups stay isolated to the primary namespace exactly as before — only `t($ => $.secondaryNs.foo)` selectors now route correctly under `useTranslation([nsA, nsB])`. Fixes the runtime side of [#​2429](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2429) for the `react-i18next` default-`nsMode` case. The 4th argument is opt-in: existing 3-arg `getFixedT(lng, ns, keyPrefix)` callers see no behavior change.

</details>

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>i18next/i18next (i18next)</summary>

### [`v26.0.9`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2609)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.0.8...v26.0.9)

-   fix(types): unformatted interpolation values are now typed as `string | number` (was `string`). i18next stringifies values at runtime, so requiring callers to wrap numbers in `String(...)` for plain `{{var}}` placeholders was unnecessary friction — and could mask the real problem when a non-string value was passed alongside multiple interpolation slots (the `t()` overload resolution would fall through to the 3-arg form and report a confusing "not assignable to string" error against the options object). Typed format specifiers like `{{x, number}}`, `{{x, currency}}`, `{{x, datetime}}`, etc. keep their precise types; this only relaxes the no-format default. The `count` variable remains `number`-only

## fix(deps): update dependency i18next to v26.0.8

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Type | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| [i18next](https://www.i18next.com) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next)) | dependencies | patch | [`26.0.4` -> `26.0.8`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/i18next/26.0.4/26.0.8) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>i18next/i18next (i18next)</summary>

### [`v26.0.8`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2608)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.0.7...v26.0.8)

-   fix(types): restore the pre-v25.10.4 `ExistsFunction` shape so plain arrow functions can again be assigned to `ExistsFunction`-typed variables (TypeScript cannot infer type predicates through multi-overload assignment). Direct `i18next.exists(key)` calls still narrow `key` to `SelectorKey` — the predicate is now declared inline on `i18n.exists`. Custom wrappers that want the narrowing can type themselves as `typeof i18next.exists` [2425](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2425)

### [`v26.0.7`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2607)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.0.6...v26.0.7)

-   fix: when a plural lookup misses, the `missingKey` debug log now shows the actual plural-resolved key (e.g. `foo.bar_many` for Polish `count: 14`) instead of the base key — making it obvious which plural category was expected and missing [2423](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2423)
-   chore: drop `@babel/runtime` runtime dependency. The build no longer generates any `@babel/runtime` imports, so the package is unused by consumers. Rollup now uses `babelHelpers: 'bundled'` so any helpers that are ever needed in the future will be inlined rather than imported externally [2424](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2424)
-   chore: stop emitting `dist/esm/i18next.bundled.js`. It was byte-identical to `dist/esm/i18next.js` because no helpers were being imported [2424](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2424)

### [`v26.0.6`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2606)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.0.5...v26.0.6)

Security release — all issues found via an internal audit.

-   security: warn when a translation string combines `escapeValue: false` with interpolated variables inside a `$t(key, { ... "{{var}}" ... })` nesting-options block. In that narrow combination, attacker-controlled string values containing `"` can break out of the JSON options literal and inject additional nesting options (e.g. redirect `lng`/`ns`). The default `escapeValue: true` configuration is unaffected because HTML-escaping neutralises the quote before `JSON.parse`. See the [security note in the Nesting docs](https://www.i18next.com/translation-function/nesting#security-note-interpolated-values-inside-a-nesting-options-block) for the full pattern and mitigations
-   security: apply `regexEscape` to `unescapePrefix` / `unescapeSuffix` on par with the other interpolation delimiters. Prevents ReDoS (catastrophic-backtracking) when a misconfigured delimiter contains regex metacharacters, and fixes silent breakage of the `{{- var}}` syntax when the delimiter contains characters like `(`, `[`, `.`
-   security: strip CR/LF/NUL and other C0/C1 control characters from string log arguments to prevent log forging via user-controlled translation keys, language codes, namespaces, or interpolation variable names (CWE-117)
-   chore: ignore `.env*` and `*.pem`/`*.key` files in `.gitignore`

### [`v26.0.5`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#2605)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v26.0.4...v26.0.5)

-   fix: `cloneInstance().changeLanguage()` no longer fails to update language state when the target language is not yet loaded — a race between `init()`'s deferred `load()` and the user's `changeLanguage()` could overwrite `isLanguageChangingTo`, causing `setLngProps` to be skipped [2422](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2422)

</details>

releases.description:
## feat: draw to create new linked tasks

Adds a "draw to create" interaction to the task graph: dragging from a node's source or target handle and releasing onto the canvas opens the Tasks plugin modal, creates a new task in the vault adjacent to the anchor task, and automatically links the two tasks with the configured linking style.

- Drag from a source handle → creates a new task immediately after the anchor task in the file, with the anchor as an incoming link
- Drag from a target handle → creates a new task immediately before the anchor task in the file, with the new task as an incoming link to the anchor
- If the connection lands on another node or handle (i.e. a normal edge-connect), the modal does not open
- Only works for Dataview-style tasks; note-based tasks are silently skipped

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: improve layout for disconnected task graphs

When a vault contains multiple independent task clusters (no edges between them), the previous layout fed all nodes into a single Dagre pass, which could cause disconnected components to overlap or be placed arbitrarily close together. 

This PR fixes that by:

- Detecting connected components via an undirected DFS before layout
- Running Dagre independently on each component
- Normalizing each component to (0, 0) before placement
- Spacing components apart with a consistent gap proportional to node size

The result is that disconnected task groups are laid out cleanly side-by-side (Horizontal) or stacked (Vertical) without overlap, regardless of the number of clusters.

## fix: default empty filter for status

This pull request makes a small change to the default filter state configuration. The initial value for `selectedStatuses` in `DEFAULT_FILTER_STATE` has been changed from including all statuses to an empty array, meaning no statuses are selected by default.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: add view panel and apply filter to unlinked tasks

This pull request introduces a new "View" controls panel to the task map interface, allowing users to easily toggle the visibility of tags and unlinked tasks, as well as reload tasks. It also refactors the code to move these controls out of the main filter panel, improves type safety and configuration for hiding unlinked tasks, and updates translations to support the new UI elements.

Given this should just be like this before with no major functionality added, I'm treating this one as a fix.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: release 26 patch

Branch protection was set up wrong allowing a partial release through direct push to main. This should no longer happen. 

Pulling v26.0.1, aiming to release v26.0.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: embed Tasks Map in files

This pull request introduces a major new feature: support for embedding interactive Tasks Map views into notes via a fenced code block, along with related UI and command improvements. It enables users to insert filter presets or the current filter as embedded, configurable graph views, and includes robust error handling and internationalization for embed-related messages. The changes also refactor relevant components and styles to support this embedding functionality.

Follow up now that we have filtering, making this relate to #371 and #329.

## TODO

- [x] Documentation on new feature (incl. interface for the embed code)
- [x] Better settings manipulation (#390)
- [x] Better Tasks Map actions (#391)
- [x] ...

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: save filters

This pull request introduces a new "Filter Presets" feature to the UI, allowing users to save, apply, rename, and delete sets of filter configurations. It also refines the visual styling and usability of various UI elements, including panels and buttons, and updates icon usage for better clarity and consistency.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: improve styles

This pull request introduces a visual redesign of the Tasks Map UI, focusing on more consistent color usage, and improved node and edge styling.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## fix: starter hint upon empty graph

This pull request introduces an empty state UI for the task map graph to help improve the onboarding experience. This is done by providing users with clear feedback when there are no tasks or when all tasks are unlinked.

Relates to #371

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: Unlinked task sidebar

This pull request implements a new "Unlinked Tasks Panel" feature in the UI. It also refines the filter panel's structure and appearance, and updates documentation.

Additionally, it provides some tweaks to the docs writer agent for better compatibility with zensical.

Closes #110 and relates to #371

Marking as `fix` instead of `docs` to trigger an update and see if the new release workflow works.

Retry of #378

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## refactor: `FilterState` for task filtering

This pull request refactors the task filtering logic across the codebase to use a unified `FilterState` object instead of passing individual filter parameters. This change simplifies state management, improves code maintainability, and makes it easier to extend filtering functionality in the future. The update touches core components, filter logic, and related tests.

Opens the door to #329

releases.description:
## feat: showing dependencies and dependents for searched tasks

This pull request adds new functionality to the task search experience, allowing users to expand search results to include dependencies (upstream tasks), dependents (downstream tasks), or both. This is achieved through new UI options and supporting logic for graph traversal, as well as internationalization updates for the new features.

Closes #346

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: search filter

This pull request introduces a comprehensive search feature to the task map, allowing users to search for tasks by summary, ID, or tags. The search interface includes a search bar with suggestions, keyboard navigation, result counts, and internationalization support. The filtering logic is updated to integrate the search query, ensuring that the displayed tasks and nodes reflect the user's search input.

Relates to #325 and #346

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## feat: new edge styles

This pull request adds support for customizing the style of edges (connections) in the task map, allowing users to choose between Bezier (curved), Straight, and Smooth Step (cornered) lines. It also introduces an adjustable corner radius for Smooth Step edges, and updates the settings UI and internationalization files to support these new options.

### [`v1.0.1`](https://redirect.github.com/lucide-icons/lucide/releases/tag/1.0.1): Lucide V1 🚀

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/lucide-icons/lucide/compare/1.0.0...1.0.1)

After years of work and dedication, Lucide Version 1 has been officially **released**!. This milestone marks a significant achievement in our journey to provide a comprehensive and versatile icon library for developers and designers alike.

It's been quite a ride — especially over the past year. Lucide has grown to over 30 million downloads per week and is used by million of projects worldwide. This release is a testament to the hard work of our community and contributors who have helped shape Lucide into what it is today.

Thank you to everyone who has supported us along the way. We couldn't have done this without you!

#### What's New in Version 1? TLDR;

-   Removed brand icons, see our [brand logo statement](/brand-logo-statement) for more details.
-   Improved documentation, guides per framework.
-   Improved accessibility, `aria-hidden` is now set by default on icons.
-   Removed UMD build, only ESM and CJS now (exception for the [`lucide`](../guide/lucide/index.md) package).
-   Package rename from `lucide-vue-next` to `@lucide/vue`.
-   A modern, standalone implementation for Angular, `@lucide/angular`
-   Support for context providers in React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid.
-   Stable code points for Lucide font.
-   Support for shadow DOM in the `lucide` package.
-   Many bug fixes and improvements.

See more at [Lucide Version 1](https://lucide.dev/guide/version-1)

releases.description: ## feat: overlay widget for task count per status This pull request adds a new status counts overlay feature to the task map, allowing users to see a live summary of task statuses (e.g., Todo, In Progress, Done, Canceled) directly on the canvas. The feature is configurable via plugin settings and supports multiple languages. Credits for the idea and example to @71-MengYi in #325

## fix(deps): update dependency i18next to v25.10.2

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Type | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| [i18next](https://www.i18next.com) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next)) | dependencies | minor | [`25.9.0` -> `25.10.2`](https://renovatebot.com/diffs/npm/i18next/25.9.0/25.10.2) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>i18next/i18next (i18next)</summary>

### [`v25.10.2`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#25102)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v25.10.1...v25.10.2)

-   feat(types): `keyFromSelector` is now type-safe — the selector callback is constrained against your resource definitions, catching invalid keys at compile time. Supports optional `ns` and `keyPrefix` options for non-default namespace/prefix contexts [2364](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2364)

### [`v25.10.1`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#25101)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v25.10.0...v25.10.1)

-   fix(types): `FilterKeys` now correctly excludes base keys that have context variants when the provided context doesn't match any of them (e.g. key `some` with variant `some_me` is no longer accessible with `context="one"`)

### [`v25.10.0`](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#25100)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/compare/v25.9.0...v25.10.0)

-   feat(types): `keyFromSelector` now returns a branded `SelectorKey` type that `t()` accepts directly, enabling pre-computed and reusable translation keys [2364](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2364)
-   feat: support selector syntax for `keyPrefix` in `getFixedT` and per-call options [2367](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2367)
-   feat(types): interpolation values are now automatically typed based on built-in format specifiers — `{{val, number}}` requires `number`, `{{val, datetime}}` requires `Date`, `{{name}}` requires `string`, etc. Custom formatters can be typed via `interpolationFormatTypeMap` in `CustomTypeOptions` [2378](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2378)
-   fix(types): `FilterKeys` in selector mode now preserves non-context, non-plural leaf keys when `context` is provided, fixing incorrect type narrowing when combining `returnObjects: true` with `context` [2398](https://redirect.github.com/i18next/i18next/issues/2398)

</details>

## Changes
Modified the release workflow to use the DEPLOY_KEY secret for authentication when pushing release commits and tags. This allows the workflow to bypass branch protection rules.

This configures git to use SSH authentication with the deploy key for all subsequent git operations
The deploy key will bypass branch protection, allowing the automated release process to push directly to the main branch

Requires the DEPLOY_KEY secret to be configured in repository settings with write access.

## fix: title and task linking with more robust re-open

## Summary
This PR adds support for clickable links within basic task titles/summaries, allowing users to include `[[WikiLinks]]` and `[Markdown Links](url)` directly in their task text.

## Changes

### New Features
- **Obsidian internal links** (`[[File]]` or `[[File|Alias]]`) in task summaries are now clickable and open the linked file
- **Markdown links** (`[text](url)`) open in a new browser tab
- Uses Obsidian's native `workspace.openLinkText()` for proper file resolution

### Bug Fixes
- **Fixed deferred tab detection**: When reopening Obsidian, tabs that haven't been activated yet are now correctly detected as "already open" and will be focused instead of opening a duplicate
  - Added fallback check via `leaf.getViewState().state.file` for unloaded tabs

### Styling
- Added `.tasks-map-link` CSS class with proper hover states using Obsidian's theme variables

## Files Changed
- `src/hooks/use-summary-renderer.ts` - Added link rendering and file opening logic
- `src/components/link-button.tsx` - Fixed deferred tab detection
- `src/components/task-node.tsx` - Pass app context to summary renderer
- `global.css` - Link styling

Closes #63

releases.description:
## fix: Prevent node overlap by using dynamic node sizing in layout

## Problem

Nodes with long summaries and/or many tags would overlap with neighboring nodes because the layout algorithm used fixed dimensions for all nodes.

## Solution

- Added `estimateNodeDimensions()` function that calculates node height based on:
  - Summary text length (accounting for line wrapping)
  - Number of tags (when tags are visible)
  - Padding and safety margins
- Updated `getLayoutedElements()` to pass calculated dimensions to dagre
- Added `nodesep` and `ranksep` parameters to improve spacing between nodes

## Changes

- [src/lib/utils.ts](src/lib/utils.ts): Added `estimateNodeDimensions()` and updated `getLayoutedElements()`
- [src/views/TaskMapGraphView.tsx](src/views/TaskMapGraphView.tsx): Pass `showTags` setting to layout function

Closes #221

releases.description:
## feat: Add Task Node Menu with Delete Functionality

## Summary
Adds a context menu to task nodes with the ability to delete tasks directly from the graph view. Also includes subtle visual improvements for better node interaction feedback.

## Features
### 🗑️ Task Deletion

New three-dot menu button (⋮) on each task node
Delete tasks directly from the graph - removes from vault and updates the view
Supports both dataview tasks (removes line from file) and note-based tasks (deletes the file)
Graph re-layouts automatically after deletion without jarring zoom changes
### ✨ Node Highlighting

Subtle border appears on hover for better visual feedback
Selected/clicked nodes retain the highlight until another node is selected
Theme-aware styling (works in both light and dark modes)

## Technical Details
Menu closes when clicking outside (using capture phase for ReactFlow compatibility)
Delete callback propagates through the component hierarchy to update React state
Uses skipFitViewRef to prevent zoom-to-fit after deletions while preserving it for other operations

Closes #81

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## Note-Based Tasks Support This release adds comprehensive support for note-based tasks (TaskNotes format) alongside the existing dataview-style tasks. ### ✨ Features **Note-Based Task Integration** - Full support for notes with `#task` tag in frontmatter - Tasks are identified by their file path and displayed using the note's basename as the title - Automatic parsing of task metadata from YAML frontmatter **Dependency Management** - Parse `blockedBy` dependencies from frontmatter (supports both simple and complex formats) - Create edges between note-based tasks by dragging in the graph view - Dependencies are saved as wiki links in the `blockedBy` frontmatter field - Delete edges to remove dependencies from frontmatter **Unified Priority System** - Note-based priorities (High, Normal, Low, None) are automatically mapped to emoji display - Consistent priority display across both task types: - High → ⏫ - Normal → (no emoji) - Low → 🔽 **Unified Status System** - Note-based statuses (none, open, in-progress, done) map to standard task statuses - Status changes in the graph view update the frontmatter `status` field - Consistent status display and interaction for both task types **Tag Management** - Tags from frontmatter are displayed on note-based task cards - Add/remove tags directly in the graph view - Tags are properly saved to the frontmatter `tags` array **Star Support** - Star/unstar note-based tasks directly in the graph view - Starred state is saved as `starred: true/false` in frontmatter - Full feature parity with dataview tasks (which use ⭐ emoji) - Gold glow effect for starred tasks works for both task types ### 🛡️ Safeguards - Warning notification when attempting to link tasks of different types (dataview ↔ note-based) - Type-aware operations ensure correct format for each task type ### 🔧 Technical Details - Note-based tasks use file paths as identifiers - All frontmatter operations preserve YAML structure - Graceful handling of missing or malformed frontmatter fields Closes #109

releases.description:
## 🎉 New Feature: Dataview Format Support

### Dataview-Style Task Linking
You can now use Dataview inline field syntax for task IDs and dependencies! This provides a cleaner, more semantic way to link tasks that integrates naturally with Dataview queries.

**New Linking Style Option**
- Access Settings → Tasks Map → Simple Task Relations
- Choose between three linking styles:
  - **CSV** (Tasks plugin default): `⛔ abc123,def456`
  - **Individual**: `⛔ abc123 ⛔ def456`
  - **Dataview** (NEW): `[[dependsOn:: abc123,def456]]`

**Format Comparison**
```markdown
Emoji format (CSV):
- [ ] My task 🆔 abc123
- [ ] Dependent task ⛔ abc123,def456

Dataview format:
- [ ] My task [[id:: abc123]]
- [ ] Dependent task [[dependsOn:: abc123,def456]]

## What's Changed
* docs: update image with colored tags by @NicoKNL in https://github.com/NicoKNL/tasks-map/pull/150
* Add Dataview format support for task IDs and dependencies by @Copilot in https://github.com/NicoKNL/tasks-map/pull/142


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/NicoKNL/tasks-map/compare/0.8.0...0.9.0

releases.description:
## ✨ New Feature: Fuzzy Tag Suggestions

### Tag Input with Autocomplete
- **Smart tag suggestions**: When adding tags to tasks, you now get fuzzy-matched suggestions based on all existing tags in your vault
- **Frequency-based sorting**: Most frequently used tags appear at the top of the suggestions
- **Type or select**: Create new tags by typing or quickly select from existing ones
- **Keyboard navigation**: Use arrow keys and Enter to navigate and select suggestions
- **Mouse support**: Click on any suggestion to instantly apply it

### Improved Tag Workflow
- Tags are now suggested as you type, making it faster to maintain consistent tagging across your tasks
- The suggestion dropdown scales with canvas zoom for better visibility
- Escape key cancels tag input
- Clicking outside the input also cancels, keeping your workflow smooth

This enhancement makes managing tags much more efficient, especially in vaults with many existing tags!

## What's Changed
* Add fuzzy finding autocomplete for tag input by @Copilot in https://github.com/NicoKNL/tasks-map/pull/141

releases.description: # Enhanced Tag Management and Visual Improvements This update brings significant improvements to how you work with tags in the task map, making tag management more interactive and visually appealing. ## ✨ Interactive Tag Management * **Add tags on-the-fly**: Hover over any task's tag area to reveal a "+" button for quickly adding new tags * **Remove tags easily**: Hover over existing tags to see an "×" button for instant removal * **Real-time updates**: Changes appear immediately in the UI and are automatically saved to your vault ## 🎨 Customizable Tag Colors * **Choose your style**: Pick between colorful random tags or a single static color for all tags * **Consistent colors**: The same tag will always have the same color across all tasks * **Color picker**: Use Obsidian's built-in color picker to choose your preferred static color * **Seed control**: Adjust the randomness seed to get different color combinations you like ## 🔍 Better Tag Filtering * **"No tags" filter**: New option to show only tasks without any tags - perfect for finding unorganized tasks * **Improved dropdown**: Cleaner tag selection interface with better organization * **Live previews**: See exactly how your tag color settings will look right in the settings panel ## 💎 Visual Polish * **Pill-shaped tags**: Tags now appear as attractive colored pills that are easy to scan * **Smooth interactions**: Hover effects and transitions make the interface feel responsive * **Better layout**: Tags wrap nicely within task cards and don't overflow boundaries * **Consistent spacing**: Everything aligns properly for a cleaner, more professional look ## 🛠️ Under the Hood * Improved tag parsing and file synchronization for reliable tag operations * Better error handling to prevent issues when managing multiple tags quickly * Settings are properly organized and use Obsidian's native UI components This update makes tag management much more intuitive while giving you full control over the visual appearance of your task map! ## Examples ### Some tasks with tags ### New settings for tags ### Filtering to untagged tasks ### Removing and adding tags through the UI ## What's Changed * feat: node tag improvements by @NicoKNL in https://github.com/NicoKNL/tasks-map/pull/139 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/NicoKNL/tasks-map/compare/0.6.0...0.7.0
